post.summary:
Create a new Bluehenge session. Also returns a list of
actions to take next.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: The top-level message sent by the client for the `CreateSession` method.

post.summary:
Delete the session. Behavior is undefined if the given
TokenData is used again after this function is called.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: The top-level message sent by the client for the `DeleteSession` method.

post.summary:
Gets the data contained within a single entity identified by name.
Entities contain information about parts and other question
answering content. See Entity for more details.

Gets the data related with an image.
The actual image will be served over HTTP.

Gets the data related with an entity extraction triple for a
specific entity-relation pair, e.g. entity:"sky", relation:"has color"
Extractions contain Subject-Relation-Object sets. For example,
entity:"sky", relation:"has color", object:"blue".

Gets a single procedure identified by id.
The response returns everything you should need to be able to display the
Procedure and it's Steps and Tasks to the user.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: Input to get a single procedure by its id.

post.summary:
Gets a single task identified by id.
The response returns everything you should need to be able to display the
Task and it's Steps to the user.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: Input to get a single task by its id.

get.summary:
Returns a list of all entities.
This list contains every entity in the knowledge graph and can
be used for fuzzy matching or any other time you need everything.

ListModels returns information about the Bluehenge models
the server can access.

Returns a list of all the procedures.
This list is contains a simplified representation of the procedures,
which can be helpful for displaying a directory or table of contents.
The full details of an individual procedure can be retrieved via
GetProcedure.

Returns a list of all the trees.
This list is contains a simplified representation of the trees,
which can be helpful for displaying a directory or table of contents.
The full details of an individual tree can be retrieved via GetTree.

post.summary: Saves a note in a specific step during a procedure.

Process input for a session and get an updated session with
a list of actions to take next. This is the only method
that modifies the Bluehenge session state.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: The top-level message sent by the client for the `UpdateSession` method.

get.summary: Returns version information of the Bluehenge server.

bluehengeV2CreateSessionRequest.description: The top-level message sent by the client for the `CreateSession` method.

bluehengeV2CreateSessionResponse.description: The top-level message sent by the server for the `CreateSession` method.

bluehengeV2DeleteSessionRequest.description: The top-level message sent by the client for the `DeleteSession` method.

bluehengeV2DeleteSessionResponse.description: The top-level message sent by the server for the `DeleteSession` method.

bluehengeV2ListModelsResponse.description: A list of models available on the Bluehenge server.

bluehengeV2UpdateSessionRequest.description: The top-level message sent by the client for the `UpdateSession` method.

bluehengeV2UpdateSessionResponse.description: The top-level message sent by the server for the `UpdateSession` method.

sourceDataVersion.description:
The date when the source data was last updated.  Source data includes PDFs
and anything else used to populate the knowledge graph.  Updates to this
version imply a new version of knowledge_graph_version.

knowledgeGraphVersion.description:
The build date of the knowledge graph in use.  The knowledge graph is
generated from the source data, but can be updated or modified multiple
times given the same source data, hence the separate version.

bluehengeV2VersionResponse.description: Lists the version of the Bluehenge server.

id.description: Unique ID of the entity in the knowledge graph.

mentions.description:
The text associated with the entity. These are the names we
use to lookup an entity.

description.title: (Optional) A human readable description of the entity

location.title: (Optional) A human readable description of how to find the entity

page.title:
(Optional) The page where the information about the entity is found.
Often takes the form: "myDocument.PDF#page=17"

cobaltspeechBluehengeV2Entity.description:
Entities are one of the core information storing blocks in
the knowledge graph. They can have images or be part of
extractions. This makes them extremely versitile.
Entities are used when you want to display information not
contained in Procedures or Tree. For example, question/answering
content, details about aircraft parts, etc.

value.description:
The value of the entity based on the input text. Depending on the
Chosun model, this might not be the same as what was given in
the input string, especially if a synonym replacement occurred.
To find the original value as it was given in the input, use the
start and end index of the entity.

start.description: The index in the original text string where the entity value begins.

end.description:
The index in the original text string where the entity value ends.
Note that this index will be one past the last character of the
entity value.

confidence.description: confidence is the confidence value between 0 and 1 for the given entity.

cobaltspeechChosunV2Entity.description: An entity recognized from the input text.

diathekeV3AudioCodec.description:
The encoding of the audio data to be sent for synthesis.

 - AUDIO_CODEC_UNSPECIFIED: AUDIO_CODEC_UNSPECIFIED is the default value of this type.
 - AUDIO_CODEC_RAW: Raw data without any headers
 - AUDIO_CODEC_WAV: WAV with RIFF headers
 - AUDIO_CODEC_MP3: MP3 format with a valid frame header at the beginning of data
 - AUDIO_CODEC_FLAC: FLAC format
 - AUDIO_CODEC_OGG_OPUS: Opus format with OGG header

diathekeV3AudioEncoding.description:
The encoding of the audio data to be sent for synthesis.

 - AUDIO_ENCODING_UNSPECIFIED: AUDIO_ENCODING_UNSPECIFIED is the default value of this type and will
result in an error.
 - AUDIO_ENCODING_SIGNED: PCM signed-integer
 - AUDIO_ENCODING_UNSIGNED: PCM unsigned-integer
 - AUDIO_ENCODING_IEEE_FLOAT: PCM IEEE-Float
 - AUDIO_ENCODING_ULAW: G.711 mu-law
 - AUDIO_ENCODING_ALAW: G.711 a-law

channels.description: Number of channels present in the audio. E.g.: 1 (mono), 2 (stereo), etc.

bitDepth.description: Bit depth of each sample (e.g. 8, 16, 24, 32, etc.).

byteOrder.description:
Byte order of the samples. This field must be set to a value other than
`BYTE_ORDER_UNSPECIFIED` when the `bit_depth` is greater than 8.

diathekeV3ByteOrder.description:
- BYTE_ORDER_UNSPECIFIED: BYTE_ORDER_UNSPECIFIED is the default value of this type.
 - BYTE_ORDER_LITTLE_ENDIAN: Little Endian byte order
 - BYTE_ORDER_BIG_ENDIAN: Big Endian byte order
diathekeV3ByteOrder.title: Byte order of multi-byte data

modelId.description: Specifies the Diatheke model ID to use for the session.

wakeword.description:
Specifies a custom wakeword to use for this session. The
wakeword must be enabled in the Diatheke model for this
to have any effect. It will override the default wakeword
specified in the model.

metadata.description:
This is an optional field to provide any metadata associated with the
session. The server may record this metadata when processing the
request. The server does not use this field for any other purpose.

inputAudioFormat.description:
Format of the audio system expects to recieve. This is an optional
field, and if no value is specified, input will be assumed to be raw
bytes (PCM16SLE) at the sample rate that speech processing models
are configured to use on the server.

outputAudioFormat.description:
Format of the audio client expects to recieve. This is an optional
field. If no value is specified, the output will be produced with a
native audio format that text-to-speech models are configured on
the server.

diathekeV3CreateSessionRequest.description: The top-level message sent by the client for the `CreateSession` method.

diathekeV3CreateSessionResponse.description: The top-level message sent by the server for the `CreateSession` method.

diathekeV3DeleteSessionRequest.description: The top-level message sent by the client for the `DeleteSession` method.

diathekeV3DeleteSessionResponse.description: The top-level message sent by the server for the `DeleteSession` method.

diathekeV3ListModelsRequest.description: The top-level message sent by the client for the `ListModels` method.

diathekeV3ListModelsResponse.description: A list of models available on the Diatheke server.

diathekeV3UpdateSessionRequest.description: The top-level message sent by the client for the `UpdateSession` method.

diathekeV3UpdateSessionResponse.description: The top-level message sent by the server for the `UpdateSession` method.

diathekeV3VersionResponse.description: Lists the version of Diatheke and the engines it uses.

phoneSet.description: The set of phonemes this model uses to represent how words should be pronounced.

nativeAudioFormat.description:
Native audio format of the model. This will be use as default value if audio format
in `SynthesisConfig` is not specify.

speakers.description: List of speaker available for use in this model.

speechRate.description:
This is set to true if the model can be configured to synthesize audio at different
talking speeds.

variationScale.description:
This is set to true if the model can be configured to synthesize audio for a given
text input differently than usual by varying stresses, and emphasis on different
parts of the audio. This feature is useful for making the audio sound slightly
different each time to avoid making it feel monotonous.

v1PhoneSet.description:
PhoneSet is a set of phonemes for words pronunciation.

 - PHONE_SET_UNSPECIFIED: PHONE_SET_UNSPECIFIED is the default value of this type.
 - PHONE_SET_IPA: IPA phoneme set
 - PHONE_SET_XSAMPA: X-SAMPA phoneme set
 - PHONE_SET_ARPABET: ARPAbet phoneme set

language.description:
Language of the speaker. This can be different from model language.
E.g. an english model with different accents: en-US, en-GB, en-IN etc.

id.description:
Unique identifier of the speaker. This identifier is used to choose the speaker
that should be used for synthesis, and is specified in the
`SynthesisConfig` message.

name.description:
Speaker name. This is a concise name describing the speaker, and may be
presented to the end-user, for example, to help choose which speaker to use
for their synthesis task.

description.description:
Speaker description. This is may be presented to the end-user, for example, to
help choose which speaker to use for their synthesis task.

id.description: Unique ID of the extraction in the knowledge graph.

v2Extraction.description:
Extractions contain Subject-Relation-Object sets. For example,
subject:"sky", relation:"has color", object:"blue".

v2GetEntityImageDataRequest.title: Input to get the entity of an image

v2GetEntityRequest.title: Input to get an extraction triple about an entity and relation

v2GetExtractionRelationshipRequest.title: Input to get an extraction triple about an entity and relation

name.title: name to identify a single procedure

v2GetProcedureRequest.description: Input to get a single procedure by its id.

v2GetProcedureResponse.description: Returns all data related to a single procedure.

id.title: knowledge graph ID that uniquely identifies a single task

v2GetTaskRequest.description: Input to get a single task by its id.

v2GetTaskResponse.description: Returns all data related to a single task.

httpPath.description: File path of an image.  Should be sent to the http server to get the actual image.

caption.title: Caption of an image. i.e. "Figure 4.3: removal of alternator"

domain.description:
The domain recognized for the query. If a Chosun model was queried
directly, this will be an empty string.

confidence.description:
Confidence estimate between 0 and 1. A higher number
represents a higher likelihood of the output being
correct.

entities.description: The list of entities recognized with this intent.

text.description: The text of the query. This is helpful when an n-best list is provided.

v2Intent.description: An intent recognized from the input text.

v2ListEntitiesResponse.title:
List of all entities.
Helpful for fuzzy matching entity names

procedures.description:
List of individual procedures. For efficiency, does not
return all the information for each step, just the high-level details.

v2ListProceduresResponse.description:
List of all procedures.
Helpful for displaying a directory or table of contents.

trees.description:
List of individual trees. For efficiency, does not
return all the information for each step, just the high-level details.

v2ListTreesResponse.description:
List of all trees.
Helpful for displaying a directory or table of contents.

text.title: Arbitrary text field asociated with Entities & Relations

v2Mention.description:
The text asociated with Entities & Relations. If an entity
has multiple names, there will be one entity but two mentions.

intents.description: The list of recognized intents, sorted by confidence.

v2ParseResponse.description: Data returned from the Parse method.

name.title: User facing name of the procedure

procedureNumber.description: Number of the procedure, as defined by the manual.

additionalNames.description:
AdditionalNames of the task
Other ways to  refer to the Procedure.

prerequisitesWarningText.title: A caution that the user should hear before starting work

tasks.description: List of all Tasks inside of a procedure.

v2Procedure.description:
Full representation of a procedure, including all sub tasks and steps.
See also ProcedureLite.

procedureName.title: User facing name of the procedure

page.description: Information related to where the Procedure is in the manual PDF used to generate the data.

procedureNumber.description: Number of the procedure, as defined by the manual.

tasks.description: Information related to the tasks related to the procedure.

v2ProcedureLite.description:
A simplified representation of a procedure.
See Procedure for full details of a procedure.

ProcedureLite is useful for getting a full list of all procedures
without getting all of the underlying data related to all tasks and steps.

id.description: Unique ID of the mention in the knowledge graph.

mentions.title: The text associated with the relation

v2Relation.description:
Relations are the connections between entities. A relation
can relate to any property. E.g, in the example "The sky
is blue." the relation is "has color".

text.title: Text of a note to save

stepId.title: Step id of a note to save

v2SaveNoteResponse.title: Empty response once the note is saved

instructionText.description: Instructions of the step, pulled directly from the manual.

summaryText.description:
Summary of the step, helpful for giving a shorter summary of the instruction_text.
Not always populated.  When not populated, instruction_text should be used.

taskNumber.description: Number of the Task to which this step belongs.

stepNumber.description: Number of the task, as defined by the manual.

page.description: Page number the step is located at in the pdf.

segmentType.description: the type of the step (instruction, warning, step, note). For TTS.

image.title:
Image of the step
A URL or relative path to where the multiple media is stored

parts.title: The parts mentioned in the step

notes.title:
List of notes of the step
User defined notes associated with this specific step

v2StepData.title: Data of a step within a task

taskName.title: User facing name of the task

taskNumber.description: Number of the task, as defined by the manual.

additionalNames.title:
AdditionalNames of the task
Other ways to  refer to the task. See comment in `Procedure`

warningText.title:
WarningText of the task
A specific warning before starting the task

steps.title: List of steps of the task

v2Task.title: Data of a task within a procedure

taskName.title: User facing name of the task

taskNumber.description: Number of the task, as defined by the manual.

v2TaskLite.description:
A simplified representation of a task.
Useful for getting a full list of all Tasks without
getting all of the underlying data related to the steps.

treeName.title: User facing name of the tree

treeNumber.description: Number of the tree, as defined by the manual.

v2TreeLite.description:
A simplified representation of a tree.
See Tree for full details of a tree.

TreeLite is useful for getting a full list of all trees
without getting all of the underlying data related to its TreeNodes.

confidence.description:
Confidence estimate between 0 and 1. A higher number
represents a higher likelihood of the output being
correct.

timedOut.description:
True if a timeout was defined for the session's current
input state in the Diatheke model, and the timeout
expired before getting a transcription. This timeout
refers to the amount of time a user has to verbally
respond to Diatheke after the ASR stream has been
created, and should not be confused with a network
connection timeout.

v3ASRResult.description:
The result from the ASR stream, sent after the ASR engine
has endpointed or the stream was closed by the client.

input.description: The user must provide input to Diatheke.

command.description: The client app must execute the specified command.

reply.description: The client app should provide the reply to the user.

transcribe.description:
The client app should call the Transcribe method to
capture the user's input.

v3ActionData.description: Specifies an action that the client application should take.

id.description:
The ID of the command to execute, as defined in the
Diatheke model.

v3CommandAction.description:
This action indicates that the client application should
execute a command.

id.title: The command ID, as given by the CommandAction

outParameters.description:
Output from the command expected by the Diatheke model.
For example, this could be the result of a data query.

error.description:
If there was an error during execution, indicate it
here with a brief message that will be logged by
Diatheke.

v3CommandResult.description: The result of executing a command.

id.description: Diatheke model ID, which is used to create a new session.

name.description: Pretty model name, which may be used for display purposes.

asrSampleRate.description: The ASR audio sample rate, if ASR is enabled.

ttsSampleRate.description: The TTS audio sample rate, if TTS is enabled.

v3ModelInfo.description: Information about a single Diatheke model.

lunaModel.title: TTS model to use with the TTSReply method

replyId.description:
ID of the reply (or slot prompt) definition in the Diatheke model that
produced this text.

v3ReplyAction.description:
This action indicates that the client application should
give the provided text to the user. This action may also
be used to synthesize speech with the StreamTTS method.

cmd.description: Process the result of a completed command.

v3SessionInput.description: Used by Diatheke to update the session state.

customMetadata.description:
Any custom metadata that the client wants to associate with the session.
This could be a simple string (e.g. a tracing ID) or structured data
(e.g. JSON).

storageFilePrefix.description:
This is an optional field to specify prefix of files that will be
saved for this session.

actionList.description:
The list of actions the client should take next,
using the session token returned with this result.

v3SessionOutput.description: The result of updating a session.

storyId.description:
The ID of the story to run, as defined in the
Diatheke model.

parameters.description:
A list of parameters to set before running the given
story. This will replace any parameters currently
defined in the session.

v3SetStory.description:
Changes the current state of a Diatheke session to run at
the specified story.

v3TextInput.description: User supplied text to send to Diatheke for processing.

id.description:
Session ID, useful for correlating logging between a
client and the server.

metadata.description:
Additional data supplied by the client app, which will
be logged with other session info by the server.

v3TokenData.description:
A token that represents a single Diatheke session and its
current state.

id.description:
The ID of the transcribe action, which is useful to
differentiate separate transcription tasks within a
single sesssion.

cubicModelId.description: (Required) The ASR model to use for transcription.

diathekeModelId.description:
(Optional) A Diatheke model to use for end-of-stream
conditions. If empty, the server will not be able to
automatically close the transcribe stream based on
conditions defined in the model, such as
a non-speech timeout or an "end-transcription" intent.
When empty, the stream must be closed by the client
application.

v3TranscribeAction.description:
This action indicates that the client application should
call the Transcribe method to capture the user's input.

requiresWakeWord.description: True if the next user input must begin with a wake-word.

immediate.description:
True if the input is required immediately (i.e., in
response to a question Diatheke asked the user). When
false, the client should be allowed to wait indefinitely
for the user to provide input.

v3WaitForUserAction.description: This action indicates that Diatheke is expecting user input.

confidence.description:
Confidence estimate between 0 and 1. A higher number represents a higher
likelihood that the word was correctly recognized.

v5ConfusionNetworkArc.title: An Arc inside a Confusion Network Link

confidence.title:
A map of features that are used for recalculating confidence scores of this
confusion network arc

v5ConfusionNetworkArcFeatures.title: Features related to confusion network arcs

startTimeMs.title:
Time offset in milliseconds relative to the beginning of audio received by
the recognizer and corresponding to the start of this link

durationMs.title: Duration in milliseconds of the current link in the confusion network

v5ConfusionNetworkLink.title: A Link inside a confusion network

transcriptFormatted.description:
Text representing the transcription of the words that the user spoke.

The transcript will be formatted according to the servers formatting
configuration. If you want the raw transcript, please see the field
`transcript_raw`. If the server is configured to not use any formatting,
then this field will contain the raw transcript.

As an example, if the spoken utterance was "four people", and the server
was configured to format numbers, this field would be set to "4 people".

transcriptRaw.description:
Text representing the transcription of the words that the user spoke,
without any formatting applied. If you want the formatted transcript,
please see the field `transcript_formatted`.

As an example, if the spoken utterance was `four people`, this field would
be set to "FOUR PEOPLE".

startTimeMs.description:
Time offset in milliseconds relative to the beginning of audio received by
the recognizer and corresponding to the start of this utterance.

durationMs.description: Duration in milliseconds of the current utterance in the spoken audio.

confidence.description:
Confidence estimate between 0 and 1. A higher number represents a higher
likelihood of the output being correct.

wordDetails.description:
Word-level details corresponding to the transcripts. This is available only
if `enable_word_details` was set to `true` in the `RecognitionConfig`.

alternatives.title: An n-best list of recognition hypotheses alternatives

isPartial.description:
If this is set to true, it denotes that the result is an interim partial
result, and could change after more audio is processed. If unset, or set to
false, it denotes that this is a final result and will not change.

Servers are not required to implement support for returning partial
results, and clients should generally not depend on their availability.

cnet.description:
If `enable_confusion_network` was set to true in the `RecognitionConfig`,
and if the model supports it, a confusion network will be available in the
results.

audioChannel.description:
Channel of the audio file that this result was transcribed from. Channels
are 0-indexed, so the for mono audio data, this value will always be 0.

v5RecognitionResult.description: A recognition result corresponding to a portion of audio.

formatted.description: Word-level information corresponding to the `transcript_formatted` field.

raw.description: Word-level information corresponding to the `transcript_raw` field.

word.title: The actual word in the text

confidence.description:
Confidence estimate between 0 and 1. A higher number represents a higher
likelihood that the word was correctly recognized.

startTimeMs.description:
Time offset in milliseconds relative to the beginning of audio received by
the recognizer and corresponding to the start of this spoken word.

durationMs.description: Duration in milliseconds of the current word in the spoken audio.

v5WordInfo.title: Word level details for recognized words in a transcript

voicegenV1AudioCodec.description:
The encoding of the audio data to be sent for synthesis.

 - AUDIO_CODEC_UNSPECIFIED: AUDIO_CODEC_UNSPECIFIED is the default value of this type.
 - AUDIO_CODEC_RAW: Raw data without any headers
 - AUDIO_CODEC_WAV: WAV with RIFF headers

voicegenV1AudioEncoding.description:
The encoding of the audio data to be sent for synthesis.

 - AUDIO_ENCODING_UNSPECIFIED: AUDIO_ENCODING_UNSPECIFIED is the default value of this type and will
result in an error.
 - AUDIO_ENCODING_SIGNED: PCM signed-integer
 - AUDIO_ENCODING_UNSIGNED: PCM unsigned-integer
 - AUDIO_ENCODING_IEEE_FLOAT: PCM IEEE-Float
 - AUDIO_ENCODING_ULAW: G.711 mu-law
 - AUDIO_ENCODING_ALAW: G.711 a-law

channels.description: Number of channels present in the audio. E.g.: 1 (mono), 2 (stereo), etc.

bitDepth.description: Bit depth of each sample (e.g. 8, 16, 24, 32, etc.).

byteOrder.description:
Byte order of the samples. This field must be set to a value other than
`BYTE_ORDER_UNSPECIFIED` when the `bit_depth` is greater than 8.

voicegenV1ByteOrder.description:
- BYTE_ORDER_UNSPECIFIED: BYTE_ORDER_UNSPECIFIED is the default value of this type.
 - BYTE_ORDER_LITTLE_ENDIAN: Little Endian byte order
 - BYTE_ORDER_BIG_ENDIAN: Big Endian byte order
voicegenV1ByteOrder.title: Byte order of multi-byte data
